LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-28-2021 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WINSTON & STRAWN, LLP'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED ANSWER, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED ANSWER IS GRANTED, AS "IT IS A RARE CASE IN WHICH A COURT WILL BE JUSTIFIED IN REFUSING A PARTY LEAVE TO AMEND" A PLEADING. (BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF LELAND STANFORD JR. UNIVERSITY V. SUP. CT. (2007) 149 CAL.APP.4TH 1154, 1163.) WITH AN OCTOBER 4 TRIAL DATE, AMPLE TIME EXISTS TO DEMUR TO THE NEW AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IF A DEMURRER IS APPROPRIATE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-22-2021 Vacated

Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer
Off Calendar May-28-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WINSTON & STRAWN, LLP's Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for leave to file an amended answer is granted, as "it is a rare case in which a court will be justified in refusing a party leave to amend" a pleading. (Board of Trustees of Leland Stanford Jr. University v. Sup. Ct. (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 1154, 1163.) With an October 4 trial date, ample time exists to demur to the new affirmative defense if a demurrer is appropriate.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Amended Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories & Request For Production Of Documents & Produce Documents
Off Calendar Oct-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Noah D. Lebowitz (510.883.3977; noah@ndllegal.com) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE RAMOS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories & Request For Production Of Documents & Produce Documents is granted. I largely adopt the judge pro tem's thorough and well-reasoned report and recommendation.

Defendant Winston & Strawn, LLP shall, within 30 days, serve further responses to the at-issue form interrogatory 217.1 and document request 40 and shall produce all responsive documents. The further responses and production shall employ the comparator definition agreed to by the parties: male income partners in defendant's San Francisco Bay Area or Los Angeles offices who were assigned to litigation and practiced patent or intellectual property litigation.

If defendant asserts that any at-issue communication is subject to the attorney-client privilege or work product doctrine, it shall produce a detailed privilege log.

All privacy objections by defendant or its male income partners are overruled, as plaintiff's interest in litigating her claims outweighs them. All other objections are overruled. All discovery remains governed by the case's protective order, which should assuage defendant's privacy concerns.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories & Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Aug-14-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceeding Pending United States Supreme Court Review Of Defendants Petition For Writ Of Certiorari
Off Calendar Aug-14-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jessica R. Perry, Esq. and Alexandra Stathopoulos, Esq. of Orrick Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP, T:415.205.3087, for defendants and moving party;
Noah D. Lebowitz, Esq. of the Law Office of Noah D. Lebowitz, T:510.883.3977, for plaintiff and opposing party.

Prior to the hearing, Judge Schulman made a disclosure that Mr. Lebowitz donates his time as a Judge Pro Tem for the discovery department of this court. This fact will not affect the Court's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. No questions were asked or objections raised.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT WINSTON & STRAWN, LLP'S Motion To Stay Proceeding Pending United States Supreme Court Review Of Defendants Petition For Writ Of Certiorari as follows:

Defendant Winston & Strawn LLP's motion for stay is granted. The court exercises its discretion per Code of Civ. Proc. Secs. 128(a)(8) and 187 and stays this case through October 7, 2019. The court believes that it is appropriate to grant defendant's short stay request to see if the U.S. Supreme Court grants its cert. petition. The issue regarding the scope of FAA preemption and its effect on Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83 has been the subject of much recent discussion and the Supreme Court may very well grant defendant's petition. A stay is appropriate where "there is some room for candid disputation" and the prejudice to the plaintiff is minimal. (Reynolds v. E. Clemens Horst Co. (1918) 36 Cal.App. 529, 530.) The court determines that granting the short stay will minimally prejudice plaintiff. If for some reason the U.S. Supreme Court does not act on defendant's petition by October 7, 2019, the stay will be lifted and the litigation will proceed.

In addition, the Court ordered the discovery motion set for Sep-12-2019 off calendar.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Noah D. Lebowitz, Esq. of Duckworth Peters Lebowitz Olivier LLP, Tel. 415.433.0333, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WINSTON & STRAWN, LLP'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is GRANTED. It is undisputed that the parties agreed to the arbitration agreement. All of the claims alleged by plaintiff Ramos fall within the broad scope of the arbitration clause. For the purpose of this motion, the Court finds that Winston & Straun, LLP and Ms Ramos had a partnership relationship. However the Court finds that the provisions related to venue and cost sharing are unconscionable and will be severed from the arbitration agreement. Accordingly, the Court orders that the arbitration shall be held in San Francisco, California, that plaintiff Ramos need only pay those costs that she would have to pay if her claims were litigated in court, and the arbitrator shall have the authority to award attorney fees if plaintiff is the prevailing party and attorney fees are available under her claims.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: John K. Stewart; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR#5963, Tel. 707.246.3323.
